page: Microsoft Azure DS12-2_v2 is a virtualized server instance belonging to the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with 1.0 thread per core. Memory capacity is 28.0 GB, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core. The instance is equipped with 57 GB of local storage and does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate that the DS12-2_v2 exhibits limited processing and memory throughput capabilities. In stress-ng testing, single-core performance scored 1049.19 (bottom 25%) and multi-core performance scored 2066.58 (bottom 10%). Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read-only and write speeds recorded at 15.54 GB/sec and 11.38 GB/sec respectively, both falling into the bottom 10%. Computational benchmarks like Geekbench 5 show a single-core score of 818.0 and a multi-core score of 1474.0. However, the instance demonstrates moderate efficiency in specific workloads, such as LLM text generation for a small 135M model, achieving an average tier speed of 87.29 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DS12-2_v2 offers a high memory allocation relative to its limited CPU resources, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high compute performance. The primary tradeoff is the poor CPU and memory bandwidth performance relative to the overall hardware profile. This instance is suitable for workloads such as lightweight database operations, caching layers, or basic memory-intensive tasks where high processing speeds are not critical.
description: Microsoft Azure DS12-2_v2 is an x86_64 virtual server in the DSv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8171M processor clocked at 2.6 GHz, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 57 GB of local storage. The instance features a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core but exhibits constrained processing capabilities. Benchmarks place its single-core and multi-core CPU performance in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, with memory bandwidth also scoring in the lower tiers. However, it achieves average performance in text generation for small language models. Given the high memory density relative to its limited compute power, the DS12-2_v2 is best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as small databases, caching, and basic memory-bound applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS12-2_v2 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 57 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring low compute performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high memory density relative to compute resources for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.
